title: Display Agent Version in 'describe' command
description: The Agent Card schema includes a 'Version' field (e.g., '0.9.0' for GenUI alignment), but the a2acli currently does not display this version information when discovering or describing an agent. We should update the 'describe' command (and any other relevant output) to surface the AgentCard.Version field to the user.
close_reason: Added Version to describe command output

title: Ensure full coverage of global flags (--auth, --svc-param, etc.)
description: The official a2a CLI has some developer-focused global flags like '--auth' (shorthand for authorization headers) and repeatable '--svc-param' arrays. We should review a2acli's flags and add these to ensure full feature parity for protocol testing. See reference: github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go/v2/cmd/a2a/main.go

title: Add 'list' command (specifically 'list tasks')
description: The official a2a CLI supports an 'a2a list tasks' command which queries the server for previous task artifacts or logs if the server supports history. We should implement a similar resource listing capability. See reference: github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go/v2/cmd/a2a/list.go and tasks.go

title: Add 'serve' command for mock/proxy/exec agents
description: The official a2a CLI supports an 'a2a serve' command to quickly spin up A2A-compliant mock agents (--echo), proxies (--proxy), and shell wrappers (--exec). We should implement this capability to help developers easily test a2acli against local mock servers without writing full Go backends. See reference: github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go/v2/cmd/a2a/serve.go

title: Epic: Feature parity with official a2a CLI
description: The official a2a CLI has some developer/protocol features that are missing in a2acli. We should evaluate and add them to make a2acli a complete superset. Features include: 1) a serve command for mocking, 2) resource listing (e.g. list tasks), and 3) full coverage of global flags like --auth and --svc-param.

title: Support inline image rendering via Kitty protocol
description: The A2UI server (and other agents) may return images in their responses or UI payloads. We should investigate adding support for the Kitty image protocol (or a similar terminal image rendering protocol) so that 'a2acli' can display images directly in the TUI when supported by the user's terminal emulator.
